title: AWS SDK v3 PutObjectCommand with ACL: "public-read" — every uploaded object is world-readable

params.passDetail: The PutObjectCommand uses a non-public ACL (not "public-read") — the object is not world-readable.
params.failDetail: new PutObjectCommand is created with ACL: "public-read", making the uploaded object readable by ANYONE on the internet who knows or guesses the URL. If user uploads flow through this command they are silently exposed publicly. Uploads succeed and the app works, so it ships. Use ACL: "private" (the default) and serve objects via pre-signed URLs.
params.absentCallDetail: No PutObjectCommand construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: PutObjectCommand does not set ACL; the bucket default applies — verify it is not public.

provenance.note: The AWS SDK v3 twin of the v2 ACL footgun. ACL: "public-read" is copied from a 'host a public asset' snippet and reused for private user uploads; nothing errors, so it ships and exposes the data publicly.
